title: SOLDERS-0003 — Pubkey.from_bytes(len != 32) panics via .unwrap() on TryFromSliceError (crates/pubkey/src/lib.rs:266)
expected: a Python ValueError (from_bytes should check len == 32 and raise, not unwrap)
confirmed_build: solders 0.28.0 (target: CPython 3.14.4+ debug build, GIL on)
prior_art: unreported (to confirm vs the solders tracker)
notes: `solders.pubkey.Pubkey.from_bytes(b)` converts the input bytes to a `[u8; 32]` with `TryInto::try_into(...).unwrap()` at crates/pubkey/src/lib.rs:266. Any input whose length is not exactly 32 makes the `try_into` return `Err(TryFromSliceError(()))`, and the `.unwrap()` panics -> `pyo3_runtime.PanicException` (exit 1 + Rust backtrace on stderr) instead of a clean `ValueError`. Confirmed for len 0/1/31/33; `from_bytes(bytes(32))` is fine. Fix = check the length and raise a `PyValueError` (or propagate the `TryFromSliceError` via `?`).
